item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While everyone is thinking of the Dodgeball announcers, I'm more reminded of the GenkiBowl announcers.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Stopped. Edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ show block
– hide block

SPORTSBALL is a pretty good pasttime, I must say. But what happens if one of Sportsball's athletes was godhead himself?

I propose the following edit:

Announcer 1: Wow, it sure looks like Tim has the lead… oh! But a strong check by Bjugstad!

To this:

Announcer 1: Wow, it sure looks like Tim has the lead… oh! But a strong check by Bjugstad!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddamn it, Ross.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

What if, rather than "this shows up and people want to watch it", it's just one dude? Any TV he watches, no matter where he is, if he's turning to a channel with sports on it, it shows him this stuff. And at first, he was pissed. The Packers are playing! Who dares fuck with his football? But after a while, by the time the Foundation gets wind of him, he's grown to accept and even enjoy it, despite the fact that it's all pretty weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's the idea

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The commentary is fantastic, though reminiscent of kaktus's "Fourth and Long" and overall reminiscent of that other "extradimensional sports broadcast" SCP in Series III that happened not long ago.

If there's anything actually the matter with this, it's probably the description/effects. Compulsion is always a pretty meh effect, and as compulsions go, this one's fairly wimpy. "People start watching it" just isn't that great an effect for something that's otherwise interesting by itself. I have an idea for this article, if you've got any interest in revising it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved it. I really, really, absolutely loved it. I could literally spend all day reading about this pseudo-sports and their commentaries, only stopping to eat and drink. Upvoted with rage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Boringdumbball

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SPORTBALL is best ball
+1 sport from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have no idea what is going on, but I'm pretty sure the phrase, "Sports for the sports god," fits right in.

+1 for SPORTS.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This needs more weird space sports, that's the fun part of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just came here to say that. Just the announcer thing, though. I really liked the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This comment parallels my opinions about this piece. I can completely believe that this is inspired by Djoric rambling on chat - it feels like the sort of piece that is hammered together around a pre-existing nugget, but which the author didn't expand on.

The 'mood' here was generic sci-fi. You don't stick to an image long enough for it to have any lasting effect. The sports are forgettable (and you could definitely have made them less so) and the descriptions of the alien cultures, species and sports shown is so fleeting as to leave basically no impression. There's very little of interest in the descriptions because the aliens have no flavour besides indulging in violent SPORT. They're cartoons.

The commentary, meanwhile, is not very effective. I see that what you're going for here is an attempt to create unease (or at least interest) by combining the ominous ramblings of K'landu with the sports-commentator format, register and speaking style. The combination of the highly-specific modern speech style with ominous word salad is a pretty solid combination, and this is reflected by the way in which it's been repeatedly used on the site. The commentary in this is just not strong enough to carry the article. K'landu's speech is pretty generic alien talk. It's not even particularly consistent alien-talk; half the time, he seems like Morbo from Futurama, ranting at the players, and the other half he's an eldritch doom-prophet. Compare it with the other spooky-talking sports-entity, SCP-1291:

K'landu: The babes shall indeed bathe in the blood of the futile dead tonight, Steve. Swiftly and silently, all shall perish.

Roy Wilson, Daemonhost: In my head there is a sick silk nail.

I don't know about you, but I think Wilson's taking home the trophy for most impressively creepy sports-entity. His line is one of my absolute favourite cryptic sayings on the site and is one of the few lines in the site that I can quote verbatim after having not read it in over a year. Unlike K'landu, it doesn't read like generic prophecies of doom. It's highly specific, very unconventional imagery with an intrinsic sort of poetry to it. K'landu's speech uses phrases and cliches that have been a staple of the creepy prophecy for a very long time. What's more, it doesn't seem to sync up with the 'aliens' angle very well.

Steve, meanwhile, doesn't get enough face time to make an impression. The strongest of the commentaries are the two where there's some element of commentator's patter and back-and-forth between them that allows the two elements to be juxtaposed. Steve should be filling the role of the pseudo-straightman: providing something the audience can get a handle on, then subverting it by being just as strange as K'landu, just wrapped up in a more digestible exterior. As it is, K'landu's inconsistent in how strange he's supposed to be, and Steve is stuck in pointless-boring-exposition role in the first log, and doesn't appear in two of them.

I think this could be substantially expanded upon - it lacks depth both in the content the broadcast is supposed to be delivering, and the style and volume of the commentary. It's a whistlestop tour through an alien culture that goes too quickly to be memorable, and reads like it was written too quickly to be memorable, either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm upvoting, but only because I think I'm seeing something in it that might not be intended. It feels to me like it's not actually an alien thing, but something made by an extremely intelligent human who doesn't like sports as a 'screw you' to sports fans. "Ha, it looks this dumb to us!" sort of thing.

And while those types of people piss me off, I'm reading the SCP asthere being oneof them behind it, and thus, am amused.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've read this one a few times, and every time I like it a little more. I'm starting to form a headcanon where this is linked to SCP-2049 as part of a GOI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall a solid "Meh."

However, the announcers reminded me of Linda von Schoonhoven and Morbo the Ahnilator from Futurama…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like others said, this doesn't really stand out to me in any particular way. Adding a Sarlacc to a hockey game doesn't make a interesting SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
changes included larger nets, the inclusion of two pucks, a shorter time limit than standard NHL games, and the placing live gigantic organisms underneath the ice which would grab and pull athletes and spectators underneath the ice without warning.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thirded, with the added bonus that most of that commentary was shit I came up with in chat and you barely expanded upon it or modified it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now see, that just reminded me of the two announcers from Dodgeball.

Cotton: It appears that Average Joe's is forfeiting the final match.
Pepper: That's a bold strategy, Cotton. Let's see if it pays off for 'em.

Cotton: It appears that Peter La Fleur has blindfolded himself.
Pepper: Yeah, he will not be able to see very well, Cotton.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was pretty much in the same boat as Crayne and Scantron…

but this clinched my upvote.

Announcer 2: The babes shall indeed bathe in the blood of the futile dead tonight, Steve. Swiftly and silently, all shall perish.
Announcer 1: That's terrific.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
never thought that someone could claim an scp from the 1000 series
nice scp +1
and also happy hollidays…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to agree with Scantron here. This just feels like it's comfortably rehashing a concept other articles pioneered, without actually adding anything interesting to the mix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a thoroughly non-memorable magic broadcast type thing. The details of the actual athletic events didn't stand out, and the commentary was generic alien talk. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't even like sports and I find this amusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oh god what is this i am not good with SPORT

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good ol' classic feel to this. I'm diggin' it.

E: Ehhhh… I'm not feeling it that much anymore. :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I couldn't sleep so I wrote an SCP. Happy holidays everyone, and may your festivus activities be enhanced by the wonders of SPORT!
